briefdescription.para: Pointer to the LittleFS context (LittleFS instance)

detaileddescription.para: Structure to hold the mount configuration for FlashFS This structure is used to configure and mount the FlashFS file system.
Pointer to the FlashFS configuration structure This structure holds various configuration settings for LittleFS.

briefdescription.para: Public constructor for the FlashFS class, enforcing the singleton pattern.

detaileddescription.para: This constructor creates a new FlashFS instance or retrieves an existing one if it already exists, following the singleton pattern. It allows providing a name for the FlashFS instance and an optional mount point.

parameterdescription.para: A unique name identifier for the FlashFS instance (default is "??").
A pointer to a file system mount structure (default is nullptr).

briefdescription.para: Deleted default constructor for the FlashFS class.

detaileddescription.para: The default constructor is deleted to prevent accidental instantiation of FlashFS without proper parameters, as the class enforces the singleton pattern.
The destructor cleans up resources associated with the FlashFS instance, ensuring proper cleanup and deallocation when the instance is no longer needed.

Mounts the file system using the provided mount configuration.
mp
A pointer to the mount configuration structure.
Returns 0 on success, or a negative error code on failure.

Unmounts the currently mounted file system.
Returns 0 on success, or a negative error code on failure.

Removes a file from the FlashFS file system.
path
The path of the file to be removed.
Returns 0 on success, or a negative error code on failure.

Renames a file in the FlashFS file system.
path
The path of the file to be renamed.
newpath
The desired new name for the file.
Returns 0 on success, or a negative error code on failure.

Creates a new directory in the file system.
path
The path of the directory to be created.
mode
The permissions of the newly created directory (optional, defaults to 0).
Returns 0 on success, or a negative error code on failure.

Gets file or directory information from the file system.
path
The path of the file or directory to retrieve information for.
st
A pointer to the fs_dirent structure to store the information.
Returns 0 on success, or a negative error code on failure.

Gets file system statistics for the specified path.
path
The path of the file or directory to retrieve file system statistics for.
buf
A pointer to the fs_statvfs structure to store the statistics.
Returns 0 on success, or a negative error code on failure.
Opens a file in the file system.

parameterdescription.para: The path of the file to be opened.
The flags specifying the file open mode.

simplesect.para: Returns a pointer to the opened file, or NULL on failure.

detaileddescription.para: Closes a file in the file system.

parameterdescription.para: A pointer to the fs_file_t structure representing the file to be closed.

simplesect.para: Returns 0 on success, or a negative error code on failure.

detaileddescription.para: Reads data from a file in the file system.

parameterdescription.para: A pointer to the fs_file_t structure representing the file to read from.
A pointer to the buffer where the read data will be stored.
The number of bytes to read from the file.

simplesect.para: Returns the number of bytes read on success, or a negative error code on failure.

detaileddescription.para: Writes data to a file in the file system.

parameterdescription.para: A pointer to the fs_file_t structure representing the file to write to.
A pointer to the data buffer containing the data to be written.
The number of bytes to write to the file.

simplesect.para: Returns the number of bytes written on success, or a negative error code on failure.

detaileddescription.para: Synchronizes the file data to the storage device.

parameterdescription.para: A pointer to the fs_file_t structure representing the file to sync.

simplesect.para: Returns 0 on success, or a negative error code on failure.

detaileddescription.para: Seeks to a specified position in a file.

parameterdescription.para: A pointer to the fs_file_t structure representing the file to seek within.
The offset to move the file pointer.
The reference position for the offset (SEEK_SET, SEEK_CUR, or SEEK_END).

simplesect.para: Returns the new file pointer position on success, or -1 on failure.

detaileddescription.para: Retrieves the current file pointer position in a file.

parameterdescription.para: A pointer to the fs_file_t structure representing the file.

simplesect.para: Returns the current file pointer position on success, or -1 on failure.

detaileddescription.para: Retrieves the size of a file in bytes.

parameterdescription.para: A pointer to the fs_file_t structure representing the file.

simplesect.para: Returns the size of the file on success, or -1 on failure.

detaileddescription.para: Truncates a file to a specified length.

parameterdescription.para: A pointer to the fs_file_t structure representing the file to truncate.
The new length of the file after truncation.

simplesect.para: Returns 0 on success, or a negative error code on failure.

detaileddescription.para: Checks if a file exists in the file system.

parameterdescription.para: The path of the file to check.

simplesect.para: Returns true if the file exists, false otherwise.

detaileddescription.para: Function Related to Directories Opens a directory in the file system.

parameterdescription.para: The path of the directory to be opened.

simplesect.para: Returns a pointer to the fs_dir_t structure representing the opened directory, or NULL on failure.

detaileddescription.para: Closes a directory.

parameterdescription.para: A pointer to the fs_dir_t structure representing the directory to be closed.

simplesect.para: Returns 0 on success, or a negative error code on failure.

detaileddescription.para: Reads the next entry from a directory.

parameterdescription.para: A pointer to the fs_dir_t structure representing the directory to read from.
A pointer to the fs_dirent structure to store the directory entry information.

simplesect.para: Returns the number of bytes read on success, 0 if the end of the directory is reached, or a negative error code on failure.

detaileddescription.para: Sets the directory stream position.

parameterdescription.para: A pointer to the fs_dir_t structure representing the directory.
The new position within the directory stream.

detaileddescription.para: Retrieves the current directory stream position.

parameterdescription.para: A pointer to the fs_dir_t structure representing the directory.

simplesect.para: Returns the current position within the directory stream on success, or -1 on failure.

detaileddescription.para: Resets the directory stream position to the beginning.

parameterdescription.para: A pointer to the fs_dir_t structure representing the directory.

simplesect.para: Returns 0 on success, or a negative error code on failure.

detaileddescription.para: Lists the contents of a directory.

parameterdescription.para: The path of the directory to list.

simplesect.para: Returns 0 on success, or a negative error code on failure.

detaileddescription.para: Lists the contents of a directory and all subdirectories.

parameterdescription.para: The path of the directory to list.

simplesect.para: Returns 0 on success, or a negative error code on failure.

detaileddescription.para:
Gets file system statistics and file tree for the specified path.
path
The path of the file or directory to retrieve file system statistics for.
A pointer to a static char array which stores the statistics and file informations (name and size in bytes) in JSON format.
Function to retrieve the available space in blocks. The available space will be printed out with debug message

briefdescription.para: The inst function to retrieve the exclusiv only instance of the class.

detaileddescription.para:
Returns a pointer to the class instance
In case there is no instance created, the constructor will be called to create the instance. Finally a pointer to the instance is returned.

Converts FlashFS error codes to standard POSIX error codes.
err
The FlashFS error code to convert.
Returns the corresponding POSIX error code.

briefdescription.para: Erase function for file system. This function erases the file system on the specified mount point.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the file system mount structure.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or an error code on failure.

briefdescription.para: Format function for file system. This function formats the file system on the specified mount point.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the file system mount structure.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or an error code on failure.

briefdescription.para: Callback function for console input command 'ls'.

detaileddescription.para: The function print out the complete content of the file system by callling member function file_tree with root directory as input folder.

briefdescription.para: Callback function for console input command 'fs'.

detaileddescription.para: The function retrieve the status of the file system by calling member function statvfs and print status information via debug output at warning level.

briefdescription.para: Callback function for console input command 'del'.

detaileddescription.para: The function clear the file system by calling the member functions unmount and eraseFs. Finally the function performs a reset with the call of function performSwReset provided by class ResetCtrl.

briefdescription.para: Represents the FlashFS file system for embedded systems with limited resources.

detaileddescription.para: Name: class FlashFS Purpose: The FlashFS class provides an interface to interact with the FlashFS file system on embedded devices. It abstracts file system operations, such as mounting, unmounting, file and directory operations, error handling, and efficient utilization of flash memory. This class structure is adapted so, a Developers can use this class to manage files and directories in the FlashFS file system without dealing with low-level details of c functions.
